notes: Launched fall 1994; introduced SMP, VRMF versioning, JFS; successor versions AIX 5L, 6, 7 remain active through AIX 7.3
Frozen in 1995; required frequent PTFs; officially superseded by AIX 4
RISC workstation/server line; rebranded to IBM eServer pSeries in 2000
Key AIX 4 feature; AIX 4's SMP support was primary headline feature at launch
Clustering solution for AIX; improved significantly with AIX 4 compatibility; now IBM PowerHA
Biweekly patch mechanism for AIX 3.2.5; replaced by VRMF in AIX 4
AIX 4's modular versioning scheme; replaced PTF chaos with structured update model
Next-gen CPU for RS/6000; users delaying AIX upgrade were coupling OS+HW upgrade — a high-risk approach per Aberdeen
